title: Quadribractea moluccana
taxonomy: Quadribractea moluccana is a species of moth in the family Noctuidae. It is classified as a noctuid moth, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of Lepidoptera. The family Noctuidae is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Catocalinae subfamily to which Quadribractea moluccana belongs. This subfamily consists of moths commonly known as "cutworms" or "owlet moths." The classification of Quadribractea moluccana reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the broader context of Lepidoptera.

intro: Quadribractea moluccana, also known as the Moluccan tree, is a species of plant that belongs to the family Rutaceae and is native to the Moluccas Islands in Indonesia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds. The bioactive constituents of Quadribractea moluccana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include extracts, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory infections and skin disorders.
